hops.context: To address the resulting mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem, we exploit its hierarchical structure and decompose it into an inner-layer subproblem for computational resource allocation and ISAC beamforming, and an outer-layer subproblem for DNN partitioning.

To address the resulting mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem, we exploit its hierarchical structure and decompose it into an inner-layer subproblem for computational resource allocation and ISAC beamforming, and an outer-layer subproblem for DNN partitioning.
We formulate a latency minimization problem through jointly optimizing the DNN partitioning strategy, computational resource allocation, and ISAC beamforming vectors.

question: In the decomposition of a mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem, what does the inner-layer subproblem primarily address?

options.A: It primarily addresses the allocation of computational resources and ISAC beamforming vectors.
options.B: It primarily addresses the partitioning of deep neural networks (DNNs) to minimize latency.
options.C: It primarily deals with solving the outer-layer problems related to system maintenance and update cycles.
options.D: It primarily focuses on optimizing the latency through external network integration strategies.

explanation: The inner-layer subproblem of the MINLP problem specifically solves for computational resource allocation and ISAC beamforming, as mentioned in the paper. This is directly related to how resources are divided and used efficiently, in contrast to the outer-layer subproblem that deals with DNN partitioning. Options A, C, and D introduce unrelated elements or misrepresent the focus of the inner-layer subproblem.
context: To address the resulting mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem, we exploit its hierarchical structure and decompose it into an inner-layer subproblem for computational resource allocation and ISAC beamforming, and an outer-layer subproblem for DNN partitioning. | To address the resulting mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem, we exploit its hierarchical structure and decompose it into an inner-layer subproblem for computational resource allocation and ISAC beamforming, and an outer-layer subproblem for DNN partitioning. | We formulate a latency minimization problem through jointly optimizing the DNN partitioning strategy, computational resource allocation, and ISAC beamforming vectors.

hops.context: To validate the superiority of the proposed scheme, we compare it against the following three benchmark schemes under the same configuration.
To validate the superiority of the proposed scheme, we compare it against the following three benchmark schemes under the same configuration.
Cloud-Edge-Device Three-Tier Scheme without DNN Partitioning (CED-WDP): In this three-tier ISCC framework, the DNN model is executed as a whole without being partitioned.

question: In the context of comparing different execution schemes for ISCC frameworks, which method does not employ partitioning of the DNN model?

options.A: Cloud-Edge-Device Three-Tier Scheme with DNN Partitioning (CED-DP) partitions the model across different layers.
options.B: Local Execution Scheme fully relies on local processing without distributing the DNN model.
options.C: Edge-Device Distributed Processing (EDDP) partitions the DNN model between edge devices only.
options.D: Cloud-Edge-Device Three-Tier Scheme without DNN Partitioning (CED-WDP) executes the model as a whole.

explanation: The correct answer is A because the Cloud-Edge-Device Three-Tier Scheme without DNN Partitioning (CED-WDP) is explicitly mentioned as executing the DNN model as a whole without partitioning, in contrast to schemes that involve partitioning or distribution of the model.
context: To validate the superiority of the proposed scheme, we compare it against the following three benchmark schemes under the same configuration. | To validate the superiority of the proposed scheme, we compare it against the following three benchmark schemes under the same configuration. | Cloud-Edge-Device Three-Tier Scheme without DNN Partitioning (CED-WDP): In this three-tier ISCC framework, the DNN model is executed as a whole without being partitioned.

hops.context: In this section, we evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed three-tier DNN partitioning scheme.

question: In the evaluation of the three-tier DNN partitioning scheme, what role does the BS serve in the system setup?

options.A: The BS, located at coordinates (0m, 0m), acts as a computational resource in the DNN partitioning.
options.B: The BS functions as a gateway for external network access that supports the DNN partitioning scheme.
options.C: The BS is primarily used as a storage unit for data before it is processed by the DNN partitioning scheme.
options.D: The BS serves as an entity for data collection, but not for processing or partitioning the DNN.

explanation: The BS (Base Station), located at (0m, 0m) as stated, forms part of the system setup where the three-tier DNN partitioning scheme is evaluated. Given that it is evaluated on the System Setup which uses the BS as an entity, and considering the nature of DNN partitioning schemes involving computational tasks, the BS acts as a computational resource.
